Oil and Gas Field Digital Twin Full Lifecycle Management Platform and
Application Based on Meta-Universe Technology

Abstract
Oil and gas fields are often characterized by wide distribution of facilities and equipment, harsh
natural environments, difficulties in engineering construction and production operation and maintenance
management, and increasingly severe risks of violence and terrorism. There is an urgent need to build a
unified management platform covering the engineering construction, production and operation phases, and
to carry out unified control over the whole life cycle process of oilfields. This paper takes meta-universe
technology as the core to construct a virtual reality environment for oil and gas field production and office
facilities. It integrates the static data in the engineering construction process, such as engineering design,
procurement, construction, commissioning, and startup, with the dynamic data and business processes in
the production and operation process, such as Internet of Things (IOT) production, sales and procurement,
and comprehensive office, to build a digital twin full life cycle management platform for oil and gas fields,
and applies it to an oil field in West Africa. Aiming at the problem of poor network transmission conditions,
this paper researches the adaptive dimensionality reduction algorithm for digital twin high-dimensional
animation data, improved Forward Error Correction coding (FEC) and Automatic Retransmission Request
(ARQ) algorithms, and adaptive transmission algorithms based on active sampling of the "protagonist
aura" model. This ensures stable operation of the twin platform and its applications in restricted and
highly unstable network environments. In terms of equipment dynamic fault identification and predictive
maintenance, this paper adopts high-fidelity modeling and multi-case simulation technology to realize the
modeling and simulation of reciprocating compressors, and establishes a fault diagnosis model based on
machine learning and deep learning, which is capable of accurately identifying and classifying different
types of faults and providing early warning, with a fault identification accuracy rate of up to 95% and an early
warning accuracy rate of up to 90%. In general, the working mode of the oilfield is transformed from "labor-
intensive, stationed on duty, regular inspection" to "fewer or unmanned, centralized monitoring, on-demand
inspection". The management mode changes from"manual judgment + experience analysis" to "automatic
warning + intelligent analysis". The abnormality handling mode is transformed from "real-time alarm and
post-disposal" to "pre-warning and mid-control". Through the construction of the platform, it improves

Keywords: Meta-Universe, Digital Twin, Smart Oil and Gas Field, Total Lifecycle Management
Introduction
With the increasing global energy demand, efficient management of oil and gas fields has become an
important issue in the oil and gas industry. The traditional management methods rely on experience and
historical data, which make it difficult to cope with the complex and changing underground environment
and equipment operating conditions, as well as the realistic and complex production environment. For
example, the production base of a company in PetroChina West Africa has a harsh natural environment, low
social dependence, and high risk, and the production operation is in the mode of "fixed watch and regular
inspection", the operation management is in the mode of non-interoperability of data from multiple systems,
and the operation decision-making is mostly based on the decision-maker's experience.
The emergence of meta-universe technology and digital twin technology provides a new opportunity
for the whole life cycle management of oil and gas fields. By building a virtual oil and gas field
environment, managers can monitor the operating conditions of the oil and gas field in real-time, make
simulation predictions, optimize the production process, warn of equipment failures in advance, and improve
production efficiency and safety. In addition, meta-universe technology can also provide immersive training
environments to help employees quickly master operational skills and improve work efficiency.
This paper aims to discuss the design and realization of the digital twin full life cycle management
platform for oil and gas fields based on meta-universe technology. By integrating Internet of Things (IoT),
big data, artificial intelligence and virtual reality technologies, a virtual oil and gas field environment is
constructed to realize the full lifecycle management of oil and gas fields from exploration, development to
production and maintenance. The innovation of this research is to provide a highly immersive management
and decision-support environment using meta-universe technology to improve the intelligence of oil and
gas field management.
Current Status of Research
Although meta-universe technology and digital twin technology show great potential in oil and gas field
management, their application still faces many challenges. First, real-time data collection and transmission
is a key issue that requires efficient IoT technology support. Second, the storage and processing of big
data require powerful computing power and advanced analyzing technology. In addition, constructing high-
precision digital twin models requires a large amount of data and expertise, and the accuracy and real-time
performance of the models directly affect the effectiveness of decision-making.
Industrial IoT technology can realize the comprehensive monitoring of the production process of oil
and gas fields through the sensor network, which provides the basis for data collection and analysis. Big
data technology can dig out the laws and knowledge hidden behind the data through the processing and
analysis of massive data, providing decision support for managers [1]. In oil and gas field management, the
combined application of IoT and big data technology not only improves the efficiency and accuracy of data
processing, but also promotes the intelligence and refinement of management [2].
Artificial intelligence (AI) technology shows great potential in the field of fault diagnosis with its
powerful data processing and pattern recognition capabilities. By training fault diagnosis models, automatic
identification and early warning of equipment faults can be realized. For example, in the oil and gas field,
feature extraction and classification of vibration signals using deep learning algorithms can accurately

In summary, in order to cope with the current management obstacles, it is particularly urgent and
important to design and develop a digital twin full lifecycle management platform for oil and gas fields
based on meta-universe technology by combining industrial IOT, big data, AI and virtual reality (VR).
Oil and Gas Field Digital Twin Full Lifecycle Management Platform Based on
Meta-Universe Technology
With digital twin as the core, this paper builds a virtual reality environment for production and office
facilities, integrating static data during engineering design, procurement, construction, trial operation and
commissioning, and dynamic data during production, sales and procurement, and comprehensive office
operation. It forms the capabilities of business application integration and collaboration, data management
from construction to delivery, visual operation control, project implementation process management,
procurement business management and decision-making, etc., and forms a smart oil and gas field with
"comprehensive perception, automatic control, trend prediction, intelligent optimization and collaborative
operation".
Technical Route
The platform is based on a hyper-converged computer network architecture, with unified planning and
construction of the infrastructure for hosting software platforms and applications. Information system
integration technology is applied to realize the integration of the twin platform with various business
application systems through application interfaces. On the digital twin platform, rapid modeling technology
is applied to realize the twin construction of digital employees, office environments and major equipment,
the twin creation of business activities based on workflow engine, video conferencing and instant messaging
applications, and the management of models, documents, attributes and their associations of factory objects
such as production facilities and equipment based on digital delivery applications. On the basis of the
above digital mapping of production and operation, real-time data of production IoT is collected through
Object Linking and Embedding (OLE) for Process Control (OPC) interface, dynamic data of sales and
purchasing and operation management application is collected through human-computer interaction, and
data mapping between twin space and physical space is realized by using rule engine, workflow engine and
3D visualization engine.
Troubleshooting and health status monitoring services for reciprocating compressors and key machine
pumps are deployed on the hyper-converged platform through virtualization technology respectively. The
production IoT Supervisory Control And Data Acquisition (SCADA) data is collected in real time through
the OPC interface, the machine learning and deep learning equipment health status assessment and fault
diagnosis models are applied to identify the key equipment status, and the digital twin platform application
is docked through the Application Programming Interface (API) to realize the visualization display of the
key equipment status.
The platform seamlessly integrates application bandwidth adaptive scheduling with client-side caching
and fusion technology, improved Forward Error Correction coding (FEC) and Automatic Retransmission
Request (ARQ) algorithms, Principal Component Analysis (PCA)-based dimensionality reduction
algorithms, "protagonist aura" active sampling, and layered adaptive coding technology, transmission
protocols, and more. It is applied to business office digital twin application scenarios, which can realize
smooth interaction of complex 3D models in network bandwidth-constrained environments and enhance
the interaction experience.

improves the overall transmission efficiency and guarantees the transmission quality of critical data.
Second, to address the challenge of managing data transmission volume, especially when dealing
with high-dimensional information such as skeletal animation data in digital twin applications, this paper
develops a PCA-based dimensionality reduction transmission technique. Through accurate data processing,
this technique greatly improves the data transmission efficiency and reduces the network burden at the same
time:
• High-dimensional data characterization: PCA dimensionality reduction technique is applied to the
high-dimensional characteristics of digital twin skeletal animation data.
• Adaptive dimensionality reduction strategy: Dynamically adjust the target dimensions of
dimensionality reduction according to network bandwidth and data importance.
• End-to-end system design: Seamlessly integrate PCA dimensionality reduction algorithms with
data encoding and transmission protocols.
PCA as a statistical technique extracts the most important features from multidimensional data and
reduces the data dimensionality without losing key information. In digital twin skeletal animation,
this effectively compresses the raw data volume and minimizes the amount of data processing during
transmission. This speeds up the data transmission and alleviates the problem of delay and bandwidth
consumption due to the large amount of data.
The seamless integration of PCA dimensionality reduction algorithms with data encoding and
transmission protocols enables end-to-end system design from data acquisition to processing and
transmission. This integration simplifies the application process and improves system collaboration and
efficiency. The end-to-end design ensures that every step from the data source to the final reception is
optimized, guaranteeing the efficient operation of every part of the data processing and transmission process.
This not only improves the efficiency of processing and transmitting digital twin skeletal animation data, but
also ensures accurate and fast transmission of critical data in complex and changing network environments.
Finally, the "protagonist highlight" mode realizes high-precision and high-fidelity sampling of the core
content of users’ concerns through active sampling for adaptive transmission:
• Protagonist highlight sampling: High-precision and high-fidelity sampling of the core content of
user concerns.
• Peripheral scene simplification: Low sampling accuracy and fidelity for non-critical areas.
• Hierarchical adaptive encoding: Encoding and transmission of data at different levels according
to priority.
• Adaptive bandwidth scheduling: Dynamically adjust the data encoding bit rate and transmission
strategy according to network bandwidth conditions.
By applying "protagonist halo" sampling technology, the system is able to identify and prioritize the most
important data to ensure its high-quality transmission. For example, in a digital twin conference, key visual
information such as the main visuals of the speaker's voice and actions are transmitted with the highest
fidelity, ensuring that the receiver receives the most accurate information. For non-core or less user-focused
content, lower sampling accuracy and fidelity are used to significantly reduce the amount of data for non-
critical content, thus reducing the overall network burden and transmission cost.
The hierarchical adaptive coding strategy further optimizes data flow management by ensuring that the
most important information is always transmitted first under any given network conditions, while adjusting
the transmission strategy for other data according to real-time changes in network bandwidth. This dynamic
adjustment is particularly suitable for unstable network environments, such as mobile communications or
remote regional connections, effectively ensuring the continuity and efficiency of data transmission.
